output.final_deliverable:
Status: cannot complete task — no evidence provided.

What I was asked to produce
1) For three authoritative sources (one government, one news outlet, one research institution), provide for each: (a) the population figure for San Francisco, (b) the date of the data (exact year or date), and (c) a direct citation URL.
2) Identify discrepancies between the sources and explain which figure is most likely accurate and why.

What I actually have to work from
- The merged results array supplied with your mandate is empty. No source content, URLs, or data were provided in the merged input.

What is missing (explicitly listed facts I need but do not have)
For each of the three required sources (Government, News outlet, Research institution) I am missing all of the following exact items:
- Population figure (exact numeric value, e.g., "873,965")
- Date of the data (exact year or date, e.g., "2023 estimate" or "April 1, 2020")
- Direct citation URL (full URL pointing to the source page)

Because none of those items were supplied for any of the three source types, I cannot extract or compare figures, and cannot identify discrepancies or judge which figure is most likely accurate.

Suggested next steps (what I will fetch if you want me to proceed)
If you want me to retrieve up-to-date authoritative figures, I will fetch one government source, one major news outlet report, and one research institution publication and extract the required items. Typical choices I'd fetch are:
1) Government: U.S. Census Bureau (QuickFacts or Latest Estimates) or City/County of San Francisco official population estimate page.
2) News outlet: San Francisco Chronicle, The New York Times, or another major outlet that recently reported on SF population.
3) Research institution: Public Policy Institute of California (PPIC), Pew Research Center, Brookings Institution, or an academic center that publishes city population estimates.

If you approve, I will perform web lookups for those sources, extract for each: (1) the exact population number, (2) the exact date of the data, and (3) the direct URL, then compare them and explain any discrepancies and which is most likely accurate (with supporting reasons and citations).

What I cannot do right now
- I cannot produce the requested population figures, dates, or URLs because no source content was provided in the merged input.

Please reply with one of the following:
- "Proceed": I should fetch current authoritative sources and return the requested comparison.
- OR: Provide up to three specific source URLs you want me to use (one government, one news, one research institution), and I will extract and compare exactly those.

(If you prefer specific source choices, please list them; otherwise I will fetch the typical authoritative sources listed above.)
